source_governance_snapshot.title: Frequency of consumption of different species of fish, crustaceans and molluscs and the effectiveness of national advice in relation to mercury contamination
notes: EFSA published this official annex on 12 February 2026 covering the frequency of consumption of different fish, crustacean, and mollusc species and the effectiveness of national advice related to mercury contamination.
Used in Dietary MCP as current official context for species-sensitive fish and seafood review, especially for high consumers and pregnancy-related advisory questions.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Scientific Opinion on the risk for public health related to the presence of mercury and methylmercury in food
notes: EFSA adopted the mercury and methylmercury opinion on 22 November 2012 and published it on 20 December 2012.
The opinion established a TWI of 1.3 micrograms per kilogram body weight per week for methylmercury and 4 micrograms per kilogram body weight per week for inorganic mercury.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Metals as contaminants in food
notes: Current EFSA topic page summarising risk assessments and legal context for arsenic, cadmium, lead, mercury and other metals as contaminants in food.
Used in Dietary MCP as a maintained official context source for metals-contaminant governance.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Commission Regulation (EU) 2023/915 on maximum levels for certain contaminants in food
notes: Binding EU contaminant regulation that includes maximum levels for trace elements, PFAS, and other contaminants in food.
Used in Dietary MCP as a shared EU legal anchor for PFAS, cadmium, lead, and mercury food-contaminant governance.

source_governance_snapshot.title: Commission Regulation (EC) No 333/2007 laying down the methods of sampling and analysis for the official control of the levels of trace elements and processing contaminants in foodstuffs
notes: Current consolidated EU official-control regulation covering sampling and analysis requirements for trace elements and processing contaminants in foodstuffs.
Used in Dietary MCP as the shared official-control source anchor for cadmium, lead, inorganic arsenic, and mercury food-contaminant governance.
